motif_threads: the two tracks of the railway

character_arcs.goal: to preserve some possibility of a future they have been together, while being asked to forfeit the thing that might anchor that future
character_arcs.stakes: the shape of her life, which she cannot articulate under his interrogation

character_arcs.goal: to secure her consent without appearing to have secured it, so the choice remains nominally hers
character_arcs.stakes: the version of himself in which he is a reasonable man who did not coerce

story_constraints.prohibits: naming the pregnancy or the operation in direct terms

nodes.sketch: the American man and Jig sit at a station cafe in the Ebro valley; he orders two beers in Spanish; she looks at the hills across the valley and says they look like white elephants.

motifs.motif: the two tracks of the railway

nodes.sketch: the American raises the operation, framing it as simple and natural. Jig deflects, asks about the drink with licorice, asks whether they will be happy afterward.
Jig asks the American to stop talking. He keeps talking. She says she will scream. The beaded curtain moves in the breeze; the hills are still there.
the train is coming. the American carries the bags to the other side of the station. Jig smiles at him. She says she feels fine; there is nothing wrong with her.

motifs.motif: the two tracks of the railway

edges.realizes: an idle wait becomes the site of the conversation they have been not-having; the hills offer her a first figurative language while he insists on plain speech
his insistence on simplicity as an argument for the operation collapses her ability to respond in kind; the refusal to name the thing becomes unsustainable
the silence resumes on his terms (whether performatively or as continued rule); she has accepted the terms of being unable to say what she means

edges.reframes: the opening's two-beers register becomes the site of a decision about pregnancy
edges.withheld: what the couple is arguing about — the reader joins the conversation already underway and decodes it in parallel with the couple's refusal to name it

edges.reframes: the operation as circumlocution becomes abortion as the reader gradually infers without ever seeing it named
edges.withheld: the word itself; the inference is the reading experience, not a reveal the text hands over
